extra.reasoning: The property requires that no input makes (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50, condition: anticoagulated); otherwise the default sets it false. rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, condition: anticoagulated); otherwise the default sets it false. The two rec_c rules (pregnancy, prior_gi_bleed) only touch rec_drug_c and are irrelevant. When anticoagulated = true, both rec_a_from_conjunction and rec_b_from_trigger fire, with no higher-priority or conflicting rules to override them, so rec_drug_a := true and rec_drug_b := true simultaneously. This violates mutual exclusion. Counterexample: anticoagulated=true, prior_gi_bleed=false, pregnancy=false yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true (rec_drug_c=false by default).
